document: Financial Advisory and Intermediary Services Act (FAIS) 37 of 2002

sections.heading: Definitions  and  application
sections.summary: Defines terms and applies to the Act's administration and authorization of financial services providers.
sections.source_quote: 12. Definitions and application

sections.heading: CHAPTER VI
sections.summary: Outlines enforcement mechanisms including ombud office, funding, accountability, and penalties for financial services providers.

Funding of Office
Accountability
General administrative powers of Ombud
Disestablishment and liquidation of Office

Receipt of complaints, prescription, jurisdiction and investigation
Determinations by Ombud
Record-keeping

sections.source_quote: 32. CHAPTER VI ENFORCEMENT

sections.heading: (1) In this  Act,  unless the context  indicates otherwise-
sections.summary: Defines key terms including 'advice', 'financial product', 'financial services provider', and related concepts for regulatory purposes.

requirements.text: In this Act, unless the context indicates otherwise

sections.source_quote: 1. (1) In this Act, unless the context indicates otherwise-

sections.heading: to the extent that such application is regulated in the code of conduct  contemplated
sections.summary: Applies the Act to natural persons acting within official duties of the State unless otherwise determined by the Minister.

requirements.text: to the extent that such application is regulated in the code of conduct

sections.source_quote: 1993. to the extent that such application is regulated in the code of conduct contemplated in section 15(2)(b).

sections.heading: The executive  officer  and  deputy executive officer of the  Board  are  respectively  the
sections.summary: Designates the registrar and deputy registrar as the executive and deputy executive officers of the Board.

requirements.text: The executive officer and deputy executive officer of the Board are respectively the registrar and deputy registrar of financial services providers

sections.source_quote: 2. The executive officer and deputy executive officer of the Board are respectively the registrar and deputy registrar of financial services providers

sections.heading: (1) When anything is required or permitted to be done by the registrar in terms of
sections.summary: Regulates registrar's powers to extend periods, direct information furnishing, and handle misleading publications.

requirements.text: When anything is required or permitted to be done by the registrar within a particular period, the registrar may extend it for any sufficient cause
The registrar may direct an authorised financial services provider or representative to furnish specified information or documents
If any advertisement, brochure or similar document is misleading, the registrar may direct cessation of publication or changes
If a person is contravening or failing to comply with the Act, the registrar may direct furnishing information, appearing before the registrar, or making arrangements for obligations
If significant prejudice or damage to clients has occurred, the registrar may apply to a Court for an order restraining business
If prejudice or damage may have occurred to a client, the registrar may refer the matter to the Office

sections.source_quote: 4. (1) When anything is required or permitted to be done by the registrar in terms of this Act within a particular period, the registrar may on application or on own initiative before the expiry of that period, extend it for any sufficient cause.

sections.heading: ( 1 ) There is an Advisory Committee  on Financial  Services  Providers which may on
sections.summary: Advisory Committee on Financial Services Providers may investigate and report on matters relating to financial services providers, consisting of chairperson, members including medical schemes representative, product suppliers, providers, and clients. Members appointed by Minister after consultation with Board.

requirements.text: Advisory Committee may investigate and report on matters relating to financial services providers
Advisory Committee consists of chairperson and members including medical schemes representative, product suppliers, financial services providers and clients
Registrar is a member of Advisory Committee without voting power
Members hold office for period determined by Minister
Non-state employees must be paid remuneration and allowances
Advisory Committee may meet or arrange for performance of functions
Registrar may submit relevant information to Advisory Committee
Advisory Committee may call assistance to investigate matters
Registrar responsible for administrative work
Expenditure must be paid from Board funds with approval

sections.source_quote: 5. (1) There is an Advisory Committee on Financial Services Providers which may on its own initiative, or must at the request of the Minister or the registrar, investigate and report or advise on any matter relating to financial services providers.

sections.heading: ( 11 The  Minister may, on  such  conditions as  the Minister may determine (which the
sections.summary: Minister, Board and registrar may delegate powers to others with conditions, including to National Treasury, other officials, or registrar. Delegations may be amended or withdrawn at any time.

requirements.text: Minister may delegate powers to National Treasury, other officials, or registrar with conditions
Board may delegate powers to chairperson, members, or registrar with conditions
Registrar may delegate powers to other Board members, appointed persons, or recognized bodies
Delegations may be amended or withdrawn at any time

sections.source_quote: 6. (11) The Minister may, on such conditions as the Minister may determine (which the Minister may at any time thereafter amend or withdraw). delegate any power conferred upon the Minister by this Act, excluding the power to make regulations under section 35.

sections.heading: (1 ) With effect from  a date  determined by the  Minister by notice in the Guzerte. a
sections.summary: A person may not act as a financial services provider without being issued a licence under section 8. Transactions concluded after the effective date must be between authorized providers and clients.

requirements.text: Person may not act as financial services provider without licence under section 8

sections.source_quote: 7. (1) With effect from a date determined by the Minister by notice in the Gazette, a person may not act or offer to act as a financial services provider unless such person has been issued with a licence under section 8.

sections.heading: (1) An application for an authorisation referred to in section 7( 1). including an
sections.summary: Applications for authorization must be submitted to registrar with information to satisfy fit and proper requirements, including personal character, competence, financial soundness. Registrar may impose conditions on licenses.

requirements.text: Applications for authorization must be submitted to registrar with information to satisfy fit and proper requirements
Registrar may impose conditions on licenses based on applicant facts, service category, and fit and proper requirements

sections.source_quote: 8. (1) An application for an authorisation referred to in section 7(1), including an application by an applicant not domiciled in the Republic, must be submitted to the registrar in the form and manner determined by the registrar by notice in the Gazette, and be accompanied by information to satisfy the registrar that the applicant complies with the requirements for fit and proper financial services providers or categories of providers.

sections.heading: (1 ) The registrar may, subject to subsection (2), at  any  time  suspend  any  licence if
sections.summary: Registrar may suspend a license if licensee no longer meets requirements, after informing licensee and giving opportunity to respond. Suspension may be provisional or final.

requirements.text: Registrar may suspend license if licensee no longer meets requirements
Registrar must inform licensee of intention to suspend and grounds
Registrar must give licensee reasonable opportunity to respond

sections.source_quote: 9. (1) The registrar may, subject to subsection (2), at any time suspend any licence if satisfied on the basis of available facts and information, that the licensee no longer meets the requirements contemplated in section 8

sections.heading: (1 ) The registrar may at any  time  withdraw  any licence  (including the licence of
sections.summary: Registrar may withdraw a license if licensee provided false information or failed to comply with the Act in a material manner. Withdrawn licenses are debarred from reapplying for a period specified by registrar.

requirements.text: Registrar may withdraw license if licensee provided false information or failed to comply with Act in material manner
Person whose license has been withdrawn is debarred from applying for new license for period specified by registrar

sections.source_quote: 10. (1) The registrar may at any time withdraw any licence (including the licence of a licensee under provisional or final suspension in terms of section 9), if satisfied on the basis of available facts and information that the licensee- (a) did not, when applying for the licence, make a full disclosure of all relevant information to the registrar, or furnished false or misleading information; or (b) has since such issue contravened or failed to comply with any provision of this Act in a material manner.

sections.heading: ( I ) A  licence lapses--
sections.summary: A license lapses if the licensee becomes permanently incapable of business due to physical/mental disease, serious injury, death, sequestration, dissolution, or voluntary surrender.

requirements.text: A license lapses where the licensee (natural person) becomes permanently incapable of business due to physical/mental disease, serious injury, death, or sequestration
A license lapses where the licensee (other person) is finally liquidated or dissolved
A license lapses where the business becomes dormant
A license lapses where the licensee voluntarily surrenders the license

sections.source_quote: 11. (I) A licence lapses-- (la) where the licensee, being a natural person- (i) becomes permanently incapable of carrying on any business due to physical or mental disease or serious injury; (ii) is finally sequestrated; or (iii) dies; (b) where the licensee, being any other person, is finally liquidated or dissolved: (ci where the business of the licensee has become dormant: and (d) in any other case, where the licensee voluntarily and finally surrenders the licence to the registrar.

sections.heading: ( 1) The registrar may exempt a  product  supplier  who is authorised or approved
sections.summary: Regulator may exempt product suppliers from submitting some or all information required for authorization, provided they apply for exemption and comply with registrar's requirements.

requirements.text: Regulator may exempt product suppliers from submitting some or all information required for authorization

sections.source_quote: 12. (1) The registrar may exempt a product supplier who is authorised or approved under a particular law to conduct business as a financial institution and who is required to apply for authorisation under section 8, from submitting some or all of the information otherwise required from an applicant: Provided that the product supplier- (a) applies for exemption when submitting the application: and (b) complies with the requirements of the registrar with regard to information still supplementary to, but separate from, the supplier's authorisation or approval under a particular law as a financial institution.

sections.heading: (1) An authorised financial services  provider  must ensure that any  representative
sections.summary: Representatives who no longer comply with requirements must be prohibited from rendering services, removed from the register, and any unconcluded business must be properly concluded.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires debarment of representatives who no longer comply with requirements, removal from register, and proper conclusion of unconcluded business.

requirements.text: Representatives who no longer comply with requirements must be prohibited from rendering services and have their names removed from the register
The provider must ensure debarment does not prejudice client interests and properly conclude any unconcluded business

sections.source_quote: 14. (1) An authorised financial services provider must ensure that any representative of the provider who no longer complies with the requirements referred to in section 13(2)(a) is prohibited by such provider from rendering any new financial service by withdrawing any authority to act on behalf of the provider, and that the representative's name, and the names of the key individuals of the representative, are removed from the register referred to in section 13(3): Provided that any such provider must immediately take steps to ensure that the debarment does not prejudice the interests of clients of the representative, and that any unconcluded business of the representative is properly concluded.

sections.heading: (1) (a) The registrar  must, after consultation with the Advisory  Committee and
sections.summary: Regulator must draft, publish, and enforce codes of conduct for financial services providers and representatives, including specific codes for deposit-related services.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires regulator to draft, publish, and enforce codes of conduct for financial services providers and representatives, including specific codes for deposit-related services.

requirements.text: Regulator must draft a code of conduct for authorized financial services providers after consultation with Advisory Committee and industry bodies
Code of conduct must be published by notice in the Gazette and become binding on all authorized providers and representatives
Different codes of conduct may be drafted for different categories of providers and representatives
A code of conduct must be drafted for deposit-related services with a term not exceeding 12 months

sections.source_quote: 15. (1) (a) The registrar must, after consultation with the Advisory Committee and with representative bodies of the financial services industry and client and consumer bodies determined by the Advisory Committee, draft a code of conduct for authorized financial services providers. (b) The code must, after consultation, be published by notice in the Gazette and, on any such publication, become binding on all authorized financial services providers and representatives referred to therein.

sections.heading: ( 1 ) A code of conduct must  be  drafted  in such a manner as to  ensure that the clients
sections.summary: Code of conduct must ensure clients can make informed decisions, have their financial needs satisfied, and comply with statutory and common law requirements.
sections.dtia_summary: Code of conduct must ensure clients can make informed decisions, have their financial needs satisfied, and comply with statutory and common law requirements.

requirements.text: Code of conduct must ensure clients can make informed decisions, have their reasonable financial needs satisfied, and authorized providers must act honestly and fairly
Code of conduct must include provisions for adequate disclosures, record-keeping, avoidance of fraudulent advertising, safe-keeping of funds, and professional indemnity insurance

sections.source_quote: 16. (1) A code of conduct must be drafted in such a manner as to ensure that the clients being rendered financial services will be able to make informed decisions, that their reasonable financial needs regarding financial products will be appropriately and suitably satisfied and that for those purposes authorized financial services providers and their representatives are obliged by the provisions of such code to - (a) act honestly and fairly, and with due skill, care and diligence, in the interests of clients and the integrity of the financial services industry; (b) have and employ effectively the resources, procedures and appropriate technological systems for the proper performance of professional activities; (c) seek from clients appropriate and available information regarding their financial situations, financial product experience and objectives in connection with the financial service required; (d) act with circumspection and treat clients fairly in a situation of conflicting interests; and (e) comply with all applicable statutory or common law requirements applicable to the conduct of business.

sections.heading: ( I ) (a) Any authorised financial services provider
sections.summary: Authorized providers must appoint compliance officers to monitor compliance with the Act, submit reports to the registrar, and maintain procedures for compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires appointment of compliance officers to monitor compliance, submit reports to registrar, and maintain procedures for compliance.

requirements.text: Authorized providers with more than one key individual or representative must appoint one or more compliance officers to monitor compliance with the Act
Compliance officers must be approved by the registrar in accordance with criteria and guidelines determined by the Advisory Committee
Authorized providers must establish and maintain procedures to ensure compliance with the Act
Compliance officers must submit reports to the registrar in the manner and as determined by the registrar

sections.source_quote: 17. (1) (a) Any authorized financial services provider with more than one key individual or one or more representatives must, subject to section 35(1)(c), appoint one or more compliance officers to monitor compliance with this Act by the provider and such representative or representatives, particularly in accordance with the procedures contemplated in subsection (3), and to take responsibility for liaison with the registrar. (b) Such person may be a director, member, auditor, trustee, principal officer, public officer or company secretary of any such provider, or any other person with suitable qualifications and experience determined by the registrar by notice in the Gazette, after consultation with the Advisory Committee. (c) The provisions of section 19(5) and (6), relating to an auditor of an authorized financial services provider, apply with the necessary changes to a compliance officer. (2) A compliance officer must be approved by the registrar in accordance with the criteria and guidelines determined by the Advisory Committee. (3) An authorized financial services provider must establish and maintain procedures to be followed by the provider and any representative concerned in order to ensure compliance with this Act. (4) An authorized financial services provider who carries on a business with only one key individual or without any representative must submit reports to the registrar in the manner and as from time to time determined by the registrar by notice in the Gazette for different categories of compliance officers, after consultation with the Advisory Committee.

sections.heading: An authorised  financial  services  provider  must,  except to the extent exempted by
sections.summary: Providers must maintain records for five years regarding premature cancellations, complaints, compliance, non-compliance, and representative compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires providers to maintain records for five years regarding premature cancellations, complaints, compliance, non-compliance, and representative compliance.

requirements.text: Providers must maintain records for a minimum period of five years regarding known premature cancellations of transactions or financial products by clients
Providers must maintain records for a minimum period of five years regarding complaints received and whether resolved
Providers must maintain records for a minimum period of five years regarding continued compliance with requirements
Providers must maintain records for a minimum period of five years regarding cases of non-compliance with the Act and reasons for non-compliance
Providers must maintain records for a minimum period of five years regarding continued compliance by representatives with requirements

sections.source_quote: 18. An authorized financial services provider must, except to the extent exempted by the registrar, maintain records for a minimum period of five years regarding- (a) known premature cancellations of transactions or financial products by clients; (b) complaints received together with an indication whether or not any such complaint has been resolved; (c) the continued compliance with the requirements referred to in section 8; (d) cases of non-compliance with this Act, and the reasons for such non-compliance; and (e) the continued compliance by representatives with the requirements referred to in section 13(1) and (2).

sections.heading: ( I ) Except to the  extent exempted by the  registrar. an authorised financial services
sections.summary: Providers must maintain full accounting records, prepare audited financial statements, and submit reports to the registrar.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires providers to maintain full accounting records, prepare audited financial statements, and submit reports to the registrar.

requirements.text: Providers must maintain full and proper accounting records on a continual basis, brought up to date monthly
Providers must annually prepare financial statements reflecting the financial position and results of operations
Financial statements must be audited and reported on by an external auditor approved by the registrar
Providers must submit to the registrar a report confirming the amount of money and assets held on behalf of clients
Providers must ensure that money and assets held on behalf of clients are kept separate from the provider's business

sections.source_quote: 19. (1) Except to the extent exempted by the registrar, an authorized financial services provider must, in respect of the business carried on by the provider as authorized under the provider's licence- (a) maintain full and proper accounting records on a continual basis, brought up to date monthly; and (b) annually prepare, in respect of the relevant financial year of the provider, financial statements reflecting, with suitable particulars, the financial position of the business as at the last day of the financial year in question, and the results of the operations and cash flow information for the period then ended. (2) (a) An authorized financial services provider must cause the statements referred to in subsection (1)(b) to be audited and reported on by an external auditor approved by the registrar, in order to produce- (i) an audited balance sheet, including such notes thereon or documents attached thereto as may be necessary; (ii) an audited income statement, including such notes thereon or documents attached thereto as may be necessary; and (iii) an audited statement of the source and application of funds. (4) The auditor of an authorized financial services provider must report to and inform the registrar in writing of any irregularity or suspected irregularity in the conduct or the affairs of the authorized financial services provider concerned of which the auditor became aware in performing functions as auditor and which, in the opinion of the auditor, is material.

sections.heading: (1) There is an office to be known as the
sections.summary: Office of the Ombud for Financial Services Providers must consider and dispose of complaints in a procedurally fair, informal, economical and expeditious manner.
sections.dtia_summary: Ombud must consider and dispose of complaints in a procedurally fair, informal, economical and expeditious manner.

requirements.text: Ombud must consider and dispose of complaints in a procedurally fair, informal, economical and expeditious manner

sections.source_quote: 20. (1) There is an office to be known as the Office of the Ombud for Financial Services Providers. (2) The functions of the Office are performed by the Ombud for Financial Services Providers. (3) The objective of the Ombud is to consider and dispose of complaints in a procedurally fair, informal, economical and expeditious manner and by reference to what is equitable in all the circumstances, with due regard to- (a) the contractual arrangement or other legal relationship between the complainant and any other party to the complaint; and (b) the provisions of this Act.

sections.heading: (1) The  Board,  after  consultation with  the  Advisory  Committee-
(1) The  funds of the Office consist of-
sections.summary: Office funds must be sourced from Board-approved budget and other sources, deposited in a bank registered under Banks Act, 1990, and used for expenses with possible investment.

requirements.text: funds provided by the Board on the basis of a budget submitted by the Ombud and approved by the latter
funds accruing to the Office from any other source
deposit all funds in an account opened with a bank registered under the Banks Act, 1990
utilise funds for defrayal of expenses incurred in performance of functions
invest funds not required for immediate use

sections.source_quote: 22. (1) The funds of the Office consist of- (a) funds provided by the Board on the basis of a budget submitted by the Ombud and approved by the latter; and (b) funds accruing to the Office from any other source. (2) The Ombud must deposit all funds in an account opened with a bank registered under the Banks Act, 1990. (3) The Ombud must utilise such funds for the defrayal of expenses incurred in the performance of functions under this Act, and may invest funds which are not required for immediate use.

sections.heading: (1) The  Ombud is the  accounting officer in respect of all funds  received  and all
sections.summary: Ombud must maintain full financial records and prepare annual statements audited by Auditor-General within three months of financial year end.

requirements.text: keep a full and correct record of all funds received and payments made, and all assets, liabilities and financial transactions
prepare annual financial statements within three months after financial year end
have records and financial statements audited by Auditor-General

sections.source_quote: 23. (1) The Ombud is the accounting officer in respect of all funds received and all payments made in respect of expenses incurred by the Office. (2) The Ombud as accounting officer must- (a) keep a full and correct record of all funds received and payments made and all assets, liabilities and financial transactions of the Office; (b) as soon as is practicable, but not later than three months after the end of every financial year, prepare annual financial statements reflecting, with appropriate particulars, all funds received and payments made during, and all such assets, liabilities and transactions at the end of, the relevant financial year. (3) The records and financial statements mentioned in subsection (2) must be audited by the Auditor-General.

sections.heading: The  Ombud may for the performance of functions in the Office and as  a  charge
sections.summary: Ombud may hire property, enter agreements, insure, employ staff, and do anything necessary for its functions.

requirements.text: hire, purchase or otherwise acquire property
enter into agreements for performance of specific acts or services
insure the Office against loss, damage, risk or liability
employ persons to assist the Ombud, determine terms of appointment, and delegate administrative functions
obtain professional advice as may reasonably be required
do anything necessary or expedient for achievement of Ombud's objectives

sections.source_quote: 24. The Ombud may for the performance of functions in the Office and as a charge against or for the benefit of the funds of the Office, as the case may be- (a) hire, purchase or otherwise acquire property, and let, sell or otherwise dispose of property so purchased or acquired; (b) enter into an agreement with any person for the performance of any specific act or function or the rendering of specific services; (c) insure the Office against any loss, damage, risk or liability; (d) employ persons to assist the Ombud, determine their terms of appointment and, subject to such conditions as may be determined by the Ombud, delegate or assign to any such employee, including a deputy ombud, any administrative function vesting in the Ombud in terms of this Part; (e) obtain such professional advice as may reasonably be required: and (f) in general, do anything which is necessary or expedient for the achievement of the objective of the Ombud.

sections.heading: (1) The Office may not be disestablished
sections.summary: Office may not be disestablished or liquidated except by an Act of Parliament.

requirements.text: Office may not be disestablished or liquidated except by an Act of Parliament

sections.source_quote: 25. (1) The Office may not be disestablished or liquidated except by an Act of Parliament. (2) In the event of any such disestablishment or liquidation, the surplus assets of the Office (if any) accrue to the Board.

sections.heading: (1) The  Board may. after  consultation  with  the Advisory Committee.  make rules.
sections.summary: Board may make rules regarding complaints, categories of complainants, complaint types, rights of complainants, and procedures.

requirements.text: Board may make rules including different rules for different categories of complaints
Board must ensure rules do not detract from Ombud's independence
Board must publish rules made under subsection (1) in the Gazette

sections.source_quote: 26. (1) The Board may, after consultation with the Advisory Committee, make rules including different rules in respect of different categories of complaints or investigations by the Ombud, regarding- any matter which is required or permitted under this Act to be regulated by rule; the category of persons qualifying as complainants; the type of complaint justiciable by the Ombud, including a complaint relating to a financial service rendered by a person not authorised as a financial services provider or a person acting on behalf of such first-mentioned person; the rights of complainants in connection with complaints, including the manner of submitting a complaint to the authorised financial services provider or representative concerned; the rights and duties of any such provider or representative on receipt of any complaint, particularly in connection with the furnishing of replies to the complainant; the rights of a complainant to submit a complaint to the Ombud where the complainant is not satisfied with any reply received from the provider or representative concerned; the circumstances under which a complaint may be dismissed without consideration of its merits; the power of the Ombud to fix a time limit for any aspect of the proceedings before the Ombud and to extend a time limit; payment to the Office by the authorised financial services provider or representative involved in any complaint submitted to the Ombud, of case fees in respect of the consideration of the complaint by the Ombud; (c) liaison between the Ombud and the registrar, and administrative duties of those functionaries regarding mutual administrative support, exchange of information and reports, other regular consultations and avoidance of overlapping of their respective functions; and (d) any other administrative or procedural matter necessary or expedient for the better achievement of the objects of this Part, but which is not inconsistent with a provision of this Act. (2) The Board must- (a) ensure that no rule made under subsection (1) detracts from or affects the independence of the Ombud in any material way; (b) publish rules made under subsection (1) in the Gazette.

sections.heading: (1 ) On submission of a complaint  to  the Office, the Ombud must-
sections.summary: Ombud must determine complaint eligibility, suspend prescription, and follow specific procedures for investigations.

requirements.text: determine whether complaint meets requirements of rules
official receipt of complaint suspends prescription for three years
decline to investigate complaints where act/omission occurred more than three years before complaint receipt
decline to investigate complaints where court proceedings have been instituted
may determine complaint should be dealt with by court or other dispute resolution process
must not proceed with investigation unless all interested parties have been informed
must provide opportunity for response to complaint
may follow procedures including mediation and allow legal representation
must explore reasonable prospect of resolving complaint by conciliation
may make recommendations for conciliation with acceptance or rejection

sections.source_quote: 27. (1) On submission of a complaint to the Office, the Ombud must- (a) determine whether the requirements of the rules contemplated in section 26(1)(a)(iv) have been complied with; (b) otherwise officially receive the complaint if it qualifies as a complaint. (2) Official receipt of a complaint by the Ombud suspends the running of prescription in terms of the Prescription Act, 1969, for the period after such receipt of the complaint until the complaint has either been withdrawn, or determined by the Ombud or the board of appeal, as the case may be. (3) The following jurisdictional provisions apply to the Ombud in respect of the investigation of complaints: (a) (i) The Ombud must decline to investigate any complaint which relates to an act or omission which occurred on or after the date of commencement of this Act but on a date more than three years before the date of receipt of such complaint by the Office. (ii) Where the complainant was unaware of the occurrence of the act or omission contemplated in subparagraph (i), the period of three years commences from the date of the occurrence of the act or omission. (b) The Ombud must decline to investigate any complaint where court proceedings have been instituted. (4) The Ombud must not proceed with investigation unless all interested parties have been informed. (5) The Ombud must provide opportunity for response to complaint. (6) The Ombud may follow procedures including mediation and allow legal representation. (7) The Omb: must explore reasonable prospect of resolving complaint by conciliation.

sections.heading: (1) The Ombud must in any case where a matter has not been settled or a
sections.summary: Ombud must issue determinations, send copies to registrar and parties, and determinations are treated as court judgments.

requirements.text: issue determinations with copies sent to registrar and parties
determinations are treated as civil judgments of a court
determinations are only appealable to board of appeal

sections.source_quote: 28. (1) The Ombud must issue determinations with copies sent to the registrar and all parties concerned with the complaint and, if no notice of appeal to the board of appeal has been lodged within the period required therefor, to the clerk or registrar of court which would have had jurisdiction in the matter had it been heard by a Court. (2) Where a notice of appeal has been lodged, the Ombud must send a copy of the final decision of the board of appeal to any such clerk or registrar. (3) A determination or a final decision of the board of appeal is regarded as a civil judgment of a Court, had the matter in question been heard by a Court, and must be so noted by the clerk or registrar, as the case may be, of that court. (4) A determination is only appealable to the board of appeal if the complexity of the matter or the reasonable likelihood that the board of appeal may reach a different conclusion.

sections.heading: (1) The  Ombud must keep proper files and  records  in  respect of complaints as well
sections.summary: Ombud must keep proper files and records of complaints and determinations, with access for registrar and fee-based public access.

requirements.text: keep proper files and records of complaints and determinations
registrar has access to Ombud's files and records for performance of functions
interested persons may obtain copies of records on payment of fee

sections.source_quote: 29. (1) The Ombud must keep proper files and records in respect of complaints as well as of any determination proceedings conducted in terms of section 28. (2) The registrar has, for the purposes of the performance of the registrar's functions under this or any other law, access to the Ombud's files and records and may without further proof rely on a copy of any record of proceedings signed by the Ombud. (3) Any interested person may, subject to the discretion of the Ombud and applicable rules of confidentiality, obtain a copy of any record on payment of a fee determined by the Ombud.

sections.heading: (1) The  Ombud must during every year, within six months after the end of the
sections.summary: Ombud must submit annual report to Board and Minister within six months after financial year end.

requirements.text: submit annual report to Board within six months after financial year end
submit copy of report to Minister

sections.source_quote: 30. (1) The Ombud must during every year, within six months after the end of the financial year of the Ombud, submit a report to the Board on the affairs and functions of the Ombud during the financial year in question, including the annual financial statements referred to in section 23(2)(6). (2) The Ombud must at the same time submit a copy of the report to the Minister.

sections.heading: Any person who-
sections.summary: Contempt of court or interference with Ombud proceedings is an offence punishable by fine or imprisonment.

requirements.text: commit acts in respect of Ombud or investigations that would constitute contempt of court
anticipate Ombud determination to influence it
wilfully interrupt Ombud proceedings

sections.source_quote: 31. Any person who- (a) commits any act in respect of the Ombud or an investigation by the Ombud which, if committed in respect of a court of law, would have constituted contempt of court, is guilty of an offence and liable on conviction to any penalty which may be imposed on a conviction of contempt of court; or (b) anticipates a determination of the Ombud in any manner calculated to influence the determination, or wilfully interrupts any proceedings conducted by the Ombud, is guilty of an offence and liable on conviction to a fine or to imprisonment for a period not exceeding one year.

sections.heading: The registrar may take any steps  conducive  to client  education and the promotion
sections.summary: Registrar may take steps to promote client education and awareness of Ombud and enforcement measures.

requirements.text: take steps conducive to client education and promotion of awareness of Ombud and enforcement measures

sections.source_quote: 32. The registrar may take any steps conducive to client education and the promotion of awareness of the nature and availability of the Ombud and other enforcement measures established by or in terms of this Act, including arrangements with the Ombud, representative bodies of the financial services industry, client and consumer bodies, or product suppliers and authorised financial services providers and their representatives to assist in the disclosure of information to the general public on matters dealt with in this Act.

sections.heading: (1) The registrar may, when satisfied on the  basis
sections.summary: Registrar may apply to court for restraining order and remedial steps if person contravenes or fails to comply with Act provisions.
sections.dtia_summary: Registrar can seek court order to restrain contraventions and require remedial actions for affected clients.

requirements.text: Registrar may apply to court for restraining order if person contravenes or fails to comply with Act provisions
Registrar may require remedial steps to rectify consequences of contraventions

sections.source_quote: 33. (1) The registrar may, when satisfied on the basis of available facts and information that a person has contravened or not complied with any provision of this Act, or is likely so to contravene or not to comply, apply to a Court for an order restraining such person from continuing to commit any such act or omission or from committing it in future, and requiring the person to take such remedial steps as the Court deems necessary to rectify the consequences of the act or omission.

sections.heading: ( I ) Subject to subsections (2) and (3), the  registrar may. after consultation with the
sections.summary: Registrar may declare business practices undesirable after consultation with Advisory Committee, subject to specific criteria.
sections.dtia_summary: Registrar can declare business practices undesirable if they harm clients or public, with publication requirements.

requirements.text: Registrar may declare business practice undesirable after consultation with Advisory Committee
Declaration must not harm client relations or unfairly affect clients
Registrar must publish intention to make declaration with reasons and invite written representations

sections.source_quote: 34. (1) Subject to subsections (2) and (3), the registrar may, after consultation with the Advisory Committee, by notice in the Gazette declare a particular business practice to be undesirable for all or a category of authorised financial services providers.

sections.heading: (1 ) The Minister may by notice in the Gazerre, after  consultation  with the registrar 10
sections.summary: Minister may make regulations for financial services providers, including prohibitions on unauthorized marketing and advertising.
sections.dtia_summary: Regulations can prohibit unauthorized marketing, advertising, and use of titles indicating authorization.

requirements.text: Minister may make regulations for financial services providers
Regulations may prohibit unauthorized marketing and advertising

sections.source_quote: 35. (1) The Minister may by notice in the Gazette, after consultation with the registrar and the Advisory Committee, make regulations relating to any matter which is required or permitted to be prescribed under this Act.

sections.heading: Any person w h o -
sections.summary: Offence for making misleading statements or concealing material facts in financial services applications.
sections.dtia_summary: False statements in financial services applications constitute offences with penalties.

requirements.text: Person guilty of offence if they make misleading, false or deceptive statements or conceal material facts in applications

sections.source_quote: 36. Any person who (a) contravenes or fails to comply with a provision of section 7(1), 8(8), 13(1), 14(1), 18, 19(2) or 34(4) or (6): or (b) in any application in terms of this Act, deliberately makes a misleading, false or deceptive statement, or conceals any material fact, is guilty of an offence and is on conviction liable to a fine not exceeding R1 000 000 or to imprisonment for a period not exceeding 10 years, or to both such fine and such imprisonment.

sections.heading: (1) In the consideration of the quantum of any  penalty  under  section 36, the  Court
sections.summary: Court must consider penalties from section 36 when determining penalty amounts under section 36.
sections.dtia_summary: Court must consider penalties from section 36 when determining penalty amounts under section 36.

requirements.text: Court must consider any award made under section 33 when determining penalty quantum under section 36

sections.source_quote: 37. (1) In the consideration of the quantum of any penalty under section 36, the Court must take into consideration any award made or to be made under section 33, from the same cause.

sections.heading: N o -
sections.summary: Voluntary business closures must be approved by registrar to ensure client liabilities are met.
sections.dtia_summary: Business closures must be approved by registrar to ensure client liabilities are met.

requirements.text: Voluntary business closures must be approved by registrar to ensure client liabilities are met

sections.source_quote: 38. No application for the acceptance of the voluntary surrender of the estate in terms of section 3 of the Insolvency Act, 1936, of; special resolution relating to the winding-up, as contemplated in section 349 of the Companies Act, 1973, and registered in terms of that Act, of; written resolution relating to the winding-up, as contemplated in section 67 of the Close Corporations Act, 1984, and registered in terms of that section, of; or voluntary closure of business by any authorised financial services provider, or representative of such provider, and no special resolution in terms of the constitution of such a provider or representative which is not a company, to close its business, have legal force unless a copy or notice thereof has been lodged with the registrar and the registrar has declared that arrangements satisfactory to the registrar have been made to meet all liabilities under transactions entered into with clients prior to sequestration, winding-up or closure.

sections.heading: Any person who  feels aggrieved by any  decision  by the registrar or the Ombud
sections.summary: Appeal rights for decisions affecting clients by registrar or Ombud.
sections.dtia_summary: Clients can appeal decisions by registrar or Ombud to Financial Services Board Board.

requirements.text: Person aggrieved by registrar or Ombud decision may appeal to Financial Services Board Board

sections.source_quote: 39. Any person who feels aggrieved by any decision by the registrar or the Ombud under this Act which affects that person, may appeal to the board of appeal established by section 26(1) of the Financial Services Board Act, in respect of which section 26 applies with the necessary changes.

sections.heading: No provision of this Act, and no act performed under or in terms of any such
sections.summary: Client rights to legal redress unaffected by this Act.
sections.dtia_summary: Client legal redress rights remain unaffected by this Act.

requirements.text: No provision of this Act affects client rights to seek legal redress

sections.source_quote: 40. No provision of this Act, and no act performed under or in terms of any such provision, may be construed as affecting any right of a client, or other affected person, to seek appropriate legal redress in terms of common law or any other statutory law, and whether relating to civil or criminal matters, in respect of the rendering of any financial service by an authorised financial services provider, or representative of such provider, or any act of a person who is not an authorised financial services provider or a representative of such a provider.

sections.heading: (1) (a) The Minister  must, after  consultation  with  the registrar, by notice in the
sections.summary: Minister must determine fees for registrar decisions and penalties for non-compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: Fees for registrar decisions must be determined by Minister, with penalties for non-compliance.

requirements.text: Minister must determine fees payable to registrar by persons seeking decisions
Penalties for non-furnished returns may be imposed by registrar

sections.source_quote: 41. (1) (a) The Minister must, after consultation with the registrar, by notice in the Gazette, determine the fees payable to the registrar by any person, or category of persons, seeking a decision or the performance of any other act by the registrar under this Act and referred to in section 3(1).

sections.heading: The registrar may disclose information obtained
sections.summary: Registrar may disclose information to authorities for public interest, with confidentiality conditions.
sections.dtia_summary: Registrar may share client information with regulators and authorities for public interest, with confidentiality conditions.

requirements.text: Registrar may disclose information to departments of state or financial regulators for public interest

sections.source_quote: 42. The registrar may disclose information obtained in the course of performing functions in terms of this Act, unless such disclosure will not be in the public interest, but subject to any guidelines issued by the Board to the registrar.

sections.heading: The Minister. the  Board or a member  thereof, any  officer or  employee assisting the
sections.summary: Minister, Board, and employees not liable for losses from lawful actions under this Act.
sections.dtia_summary: Regulators not liable for losses from lawful actions under this Act.

requirements.text: Minister, Board, and employees not liable for losses from lawful actions under this Act

sections.source_quote: 43. The Minister, the Board or a member thereof, any officer or employee assisting the Board, the registrar, the Advisory Committee or any member thereof, the Ombud and any employee in the Office of the Ombud, or other body or person performing any function in terms of this Act, is not liable for any loss sustained by or damage caused to any person as a result of anything done or omitted by any of them in the bona fide exercise of any power or carrying out of any duty or performance of any function under or in terms of a provision of this Act.

sections.heading: (1 ) The registrar  may on or after  the commencement of this  Act, but prior to the
sections.summary: Registrar may exempt persons from provisions if they are already regulated or won't cause hardship.
sections.dtia_summary: Registrar may exempt persons from provisions if they are already regulated or won't cause hardship.

requirements.text: Registrar may exempt persons from provisions if they are already partially or wholly regulated by other law
Exemptions must not cause financial or other hardship to clients

sections.source_quote: 44. (1) The registrar may on or after the commencement of this Act, but prior to the date determined by the Minister in terms of section 7(1), exempt any person or category of persons from the provisions of that section if the registrar is satisfied that- (a) the rendering of any financial service by the applicant is already partially or wholly regulated by any other law; or (b) the application of the said section to the applicant will cause the applicant or clients of the applicant financial or other hardship or prejudice; and (c) the granting of the exemption will not- (i) conflict with the public interest; (ii) prejudice the interests of clients; and (iii) frustrate the achievement of the objects of this Act.

sections.heading: ( 1 ) The  plovisions of this Act do not apply to the rendering of financial services
sections.summary: This Act does not apply to financial services provided by certain entities including authorized users, clearing houses, central securities depositories, managers, executors, administrators, trustees, liquidators, and minors under specific conditions.
sections.dtia_summary: Exemptions for specific entities from applying the Act to financial services, relevant for DTIA scope determination.

requirements.text: The provisions of this Act do not apply to the rendering of financial services

sections.source_quote: 35. (1) The provisions of this Act do not apply to the rendering of financial services

sections.heading: This Act is called the Financial Advisory and Intermediary Services Act, 2002, 45
sections.summary: This Act is called the Financial Advisory and Intermediary Services Act, 2002 and comes into operation on a date fixed by the President by proclamation in the Gazette.
sections.source_quote: 46. This Act is called the Financial Advisory and Intermediary Services Act, 2002, 45 and comes into operation on a date fixed by the President by proclamation in the Gazette.

sections.heading: The amendment of section 4
sections.summary: Amends section 10 by substituting paragraphs for stock-brokers and financial instrument traders.

requirements.text: any stock-broker as defined in section 1 of the Stock Exchanges Control Act, 1985 [or any person contemplated in paragraph (d), (e) or (f) of section 4(1) of that Act]
any financial instrument trader as defined in section 1 of the Financial Markets Control Act, 1989 [or any person contemplated in paragraph (n), (w) or (h) of section 5(1) of that Act]

sections.source_quote: 1. The amendment of section 10 by the substitution in subsection (3) for paragraphs (a) and (b) of the following paragraphs: "(a) any stock-broker as defined in section 1 of the Stock Exchanges Control Act, 1985 [or any person contemplated in paragraph (d), (e) or (f) of section 4(1) of that Act]; or (b) any financial instrument trader as defined in section 1 of the Financial Markets Control Act, 1989 (Act 5 of 1989) [or any person contemplated in paragraph (n), (w) or (h) of section 5(1) of that Act]."

sections.heading: The amendment of section 12
sections.summary: Amends section 12 by substituting a paragraph that members must carry on business in accordance with rules and may not effect transactions with persons requiring authorization without verification.

requirements.text: a member carries on a business contemplated in section 4(1) in accordance with the provisions of the rules

a member may not effect a transaction with a person whom the member reasonably believes requires authorisation as a financial services provider or the status of a representative in terms of the Financial Advisory and Intermediary Services Act, 2001, without having taken reasonable measures to ascertain that such person has the required authorisation or status

sections.source_quote: 2. The amendment of section 12 by the substitution for paragraph (d) of subsection (1) of the following paragraph: "(d) that a member carries on a business contemplated in section 4(1) in accordance with the provisions of the rules; and a member may not effect a transaction with a person whom the member reasonably believes requires authorisation as a financial services provider or the status of a representative in terms of the Financial Advisory and Intermediary Services Act, 2001, without having taken reasonable measures to ascertain that such person has the required authorisation or status."

sections.heading: The  amendment
sections.summary: Amends section 45 by deleting specific subparagraphs and substituting wording related to financial exchanges and members.

requirements.text: but who is carrying on the business of a stock exchange or of a member [or of a person requiring approval in terms of section 41 as the case may be]

sections.source_quote: 4. The amendment of section 45 by the deletion of subparagraph (iii) of paragraph (a) of subsection (1); by the deletion of the word "or" at the end of subparagraph (ii) of paragraph (b) of subsection (1), and of subparagraph (iii) of the said paragraph (b); and by the substitution for the words following on subparagraph (iii) of paragraph (b) of subsection (1) of the following words: "but who is carrying on the business of a stock exchange or of a member [or of a person requiring approval in terms of section 41 as the case may be; and".

sections.heading: The amendment of section 48
sections.summary: Amends section 48 by substituting a paragraph that contravenes specific sections of the Act.

requirements.text: contravenes a provision of section 3(1) or (2), 4(1)[or (2)] or 14

sections.source_quote: 6. The amendment of section 48 by the substitution for paragraph (a) of subsection (1) of the following paragraph: "(a) contravenes a provision of section 3(1) or (2), 4(1)[or (2)] or 14;".

sections.heading: The substitution of the follow-
sections.summary: Substitutes the heading and subsection for section 50 to define court powers to declare members, officers, or employees disqualified.
sections.source_quote: 7. The substitution of the following heading and subsection for the leading and subsection (1) of section 50: "Powers of court to declare member, officer or employee of member disqualified"

sections.heading: (1) If a court-
sections.summary: Courts may declare members, officers, or employees disqualified for dishonest conduct in financial services.

requirements.text: the court may declare that member, officer or employee of a member [person or such person's officer or employee] to be disqualified for an indefinite period or for a period specified by the court, from carrying on the business of a member or from being an officer or employee of a member

sections.source_quote: 50. (1) If a court convicts a member or an officer or employee of a member [or a person approved in terms of section 41 under this Act or of an offence of which any dishonest act or omission is an element; or finds, in proceedings to which a member or an officer or employee of a member [a person approved in terms of section 41 or such person's officer or employee] is a party or in which [his] such member's, officer's or employee's conduct is called in question, that such member, officer or employee has been guilty of dishonest conduct, the court may (in addition, in a case referred to in paragraph (a), to any sentence it may impose) declare that member, officer or employee of a member [person or such person's officer or employee] to be disqualified for an indefinite period or for a period specified by the court, from carrying on the business of a member or from being an officer or employee of a  member.

any financial instrument trader as defined in section 1 of the Financial Markets Control Act, 1989 [or any person contemplated in paragraph (n), (w) or (h) of section 5(1) of that Act]

The amendment of section
sections.summary: Amends section 45 by deleting specific subparagraphs and substituting wording related to financial exchanges and members.

The amendment of section 45 by the deletion of subparagraph (iii) of paragraph (a) of subsection (1); by the deletion of the word "or" at the end of subparagraph (ii) of paragraph (b) of subsection (1), and of subparagraph (iii) of the said paragraph (b); and by the substitution for the words following on subparagraph (iii) of paragraph (b) of subsection (1) of the following words: "but who is carrying on the business of a stock exchange or of a member [or of a person requiring approval in terms of section 41 as the case may be; and".

sections.heading: The amendment of section
sections.summary: Amends section 48 by substituting a paragraph that contravenes specific sections of the Act.

requirements.text: contravenes a provision of section 3(1) or (2), 4(1)[or (2)] or 14

sections.heading: A record  purporting to have
The substitution of the follow.
sections.summary: Substitutes the heading and subsection for section 31 to define court powers to declare members, officers, or employees disqualified.
sections.source_quote: 8. The substitution of the following heading and subsection for the heading and subsection (1) of section 31: "Power of court to declare member or officer or employee of member disqualified"

sections.heading: (1) If a court-
sections.summary: Courts may declare members, officers, or employees disqualified for dishonest conduct in financial services.

requirements.text: the court may declare that member, officer or employee of a member [or person] to be disqualified for an indefinite period or for a period specified by the court, from carrying on the business of a member or from being an officer or employee of a member

sections.source_quote: 31. (1) If a court convicts a member or officer or employee of a member [or a person approved in terms of section 51 of an offence under this Act or of an offence of which any dishonest act or omission is an element; or finds, in proceedings to which a member or officer or employee of a member [or a person approved in terms of section 51 or such person's officer or employee] is a party or in which such member's, officer's or employee's [or person's] conduct is called in question, that such member, officer or employee [or person] has been guilty of dishonest conduct, the court may (in addition, in a case referred to in paragraph (a) to any sentence it may impose) declare that member, officer or employee of a member [or person] to be disqualified for an indefinite period or for a period specified by the court, from carrying on the business of a member or from being an officer or employee of a member.

sections.heading: The amendment of section 10
sections.summary: Amends section 10 by substituting paragraphs for stock-brokers and financial instrument traders.

requirements.text: any stock-broker as defined in section 1 of the Stock Exchanges Control Act, 1985 [or any person contemplated in paragraph (d), (e) or (f) of section 4(1) of that Act]
any financial instrument trader as defined in section 1 of the Financial Markets Control Act, 1989 [or any person contemplated in paragraph (n), (w) or (h) of section 5(1) of that Act]

sections.source_quote: 1. The amendment of section 10 by the substitution in subsection (3) for paragraphs (a) and (b) of the following paragraphs: "(a) any stock-broker as defined in section 1 of the Stock Exchanges Control Act, 1985 [or any person contemplated in paragraph (d), (e) or (f) of section 4(1) of that Act]; or (b) any financial instrument trader as defined in section 1 of the Financial Markets Control Act, 1989 (Act 5 of 1989) [or any person contemplated in paragraph (n), (w) or (h) of section 5(1) of that Act]."
